title: Androrchis × ehdenica
taxonomy: Androrchis × ehdenica is a hybrid orchid species resulting from the cross between Androrchis palustris (formerly Dactylorhiza palustris) and Androrchis aetolica (formerly Dactylorhiza aetolica). It is a type of terrestrial orchid, commonly found in the wild in specific regions. This orchid species has been classified in the Orchidaceae family, which comprises over 30,000 known species of orchids. Androrchis × ehdenica is often classified as a hybrid within the subgenus Androrchis. It is a unique example of a hybrid orchid species, which is the result of natural or artificial cross-pollination between different orchid species.
uses: Androrchis × ehdenica, a hybrid orchid, has been used in traditional medicine, although its effectiveness and safety have not been extensively scientifically proven. It is primarily found in the mountains of Cyprus and is known to have various medicinal properties. Some studies suggest that the plant may have anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. intro: Androorchis × ehdenica, also known as the hybrid orchid, is a plant species derived from the cross between Androorkhis cirtensis and Orchis italica. It has been traditionally used in medicine for its health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antifungal properties, making it effective in treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. The bioactive constituents of Androorchis × ehdenica include alkaloids, phenolic compounds, and flavonoids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant are teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to alleviate respiratory symptoms and promote overall well-being.
